- [tg_saved_searches_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List saved Telegram boolean search queries.
- [tg_timeline](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get Telegram message volume timeline data (messages per day) with severity breakdown. Useful for visualizing activity spikes.
- [threatmon_infostealer_search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search ThreatMon IntelHub for compromised credentials and infected devices linked to a domain via real stealer malware logs. Returns compromised URLs, IPs, usernames, dates, and employee/user classification. Data sourced from ~2.18B compromised users and ~10.47B leaked credentials.
- [ti_brief_sector](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Return a sector-specific threat brief (Financial, Healthcare, or Government) from the threat-intel vertical. Each brief includes an executive summary, top N sector-relevant threats with risk assessments and recommended actions.
- [ti_export_stix](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Export IOC family indicators as a STIX 2.1 bundle. Reads the IOC family body from the threat-intel manifest, converts each indicator to a STIX indicator object with pattern, and wraps in a bundle with TLP marking. Importable into OpenCTI, MISP, or any TAXII 2.1 consumer.
- [ti_get_cve](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Return the full CVE body with CVSS vector, CWE IDs, references, and (where populated) BSI description and LLM summary/recommended action. Use ti_list_cves first to discover CVE IDs.
- [ti_get_ioc](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Return the full IOC family body with indicators, MITRE techniques, context, and (where populated) LLM summary. Use ti_list_iocs first to discover family slugs.
- [ti_list_cves](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List CVEs from the threat-intel vertical (NVD + CISA KEV). CVEs are enriched with priority scoring (CVSS + KEV + recency). Filter by severity, KEV-only, vendor, recency, or keyword.
- [ti_list_iocs](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List IOC families (ransomware, malware, APT groups, C2 frameworks, stealers, phishing kits) from the threat-intel vertical, sourced from Daily-Hunt references and tracked by this Worker.
- [ti_list_kev](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Return the full CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) snapshot — actively exploited CVEs with required actions and due dates. Each entry includes vendor, product, short description, required action, and due date.
- [ti_search_malwarebazaar](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search MalwareBazaar (abuse.ch) for malware samples by tag or signature. Returns SHA-256, MD5, file name, type, malware family signature, tags, and timestamps. Tries tag search first, falls back to signature. Free API — no key required.
- [ti_search_otx](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search AlienVault OTX for threat pulses matching a query. Returns pulse metadata (name, tags, TLP, malware families, MITRE ATT&CK IDs) and indicators for the top 5 pulses. Requires OTX_API_KEY (free at otx.alienvault.com).
- [ti_search_ransomware_live](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search ransomware.live for ransomware group profiles. Returns group description, .onion leak-site URLs, recent victims (with country/sector), MITRE ATT&CK TTPs, and known tools. Free public API — no key required.
- [ti_search_threatfox](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search ThreatFox (abuse.ch) for IOCs matching a search term. Returns IOC type, value, malware family, confidence, timestamps, and reporter. Free API — no key required. Useful for looking up specific IPs, domains, URLs, or hashes against ThreatCrowd's crowdsourced IOC database.
- [ti_stats](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Return cache + manifest stats for the Threat Intel data: index loaded, KEV loaded, body-cache sizes and hit ratios. Useful for diagnosing cold-start latency.
- [tools_get](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get the full profile for a specific security tool by slug.
- [tools_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List security tools from the curated Tools Directory. Filter by category (recon, exploitation, post-exploitation, defense, detection, forensics, osint, c2, phishing, crypto, mobile, cloud, network, reverse-engineering, web, misc), keyword, or offensive/defensive scope.
- [tor_exit_check](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Check if a specific IP address is a known Tor exit node. Returns boolean and the queried IP.
- [tor_exit_details](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get detailed Tor exit node information including fingerprints, published timestamps, and exit addresses. More comprehensive than the bulk exit list.
- [tor_exit_nodes](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get current Tor exit node IP addresses from the official Tor Project bulk exit list. Useful for identifying if traffic originates from the Tor network.
- [tor_fetch_onion](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Fetch raw HTML from a .onion URL via tor2web gateway. Returns page HTML and status code. Note: uses public tor2web proxies, not a local Tor SOCKS5 daemon — for true Tor anonymity, use tor locally.
- [tor_scrape_onion](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Fetch and parse a .onion site via tor2web gateway. Returns structured data: title, links, body text, status code. Useful for extracting content from dark web sites.
- [tor_search_onion](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Search for .onion sites using the Ahmia.fi search engine. Returns matching pages with title, URL, and description. Note: Ahmia selectively indexes .onion sites; not all dark web content is discoverable.
- [tor_status](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Check the dark web access gateway status. Uses public tor2web gateways to reach .onion sites (no local Tor daemon required). Returns available gateways and method info.
- [trace_crypto_address](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Trace a cryptocurrency wallet address. Returns balance, transaction history, and associated entities from blockchain explorers.
- [traceix_lookup](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Look up a SHA-256 file hash against traceix.com (PCEF) for antivirus/reputation results. Returns per-engine verdicts (Safe/Malicious/Unknown/Failed). Powered by Perkins Fund AI. Requires TRACEIX_API_KEY secret.
- [unified_search](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:search): Cross-source search across all threat intelligence feeds. Search by keyword, IOC, actor name, malware family, or CVE to find matching entries across briefings, live feeds, ransomware data, and more.
- [username_generate_patterns](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Generate username variations for typosquatting detection and OSINT. Returns common patterns: leetspeak, double letters, prefix/suffix variations, dot/underscore/hyphen separators, number suffixes.
- [username_scrape_profiles](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Scrape profile metadata (display name, bio, avatar, follower counts) from platforms where the username is found. Returns rich profile data, not just found/not-found.
- [validate_yara_rule](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:detection): Validate a YARA rule syntax. Checks for balanced braces, required sections, and proper string definitions.
- [virushee_check](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Check a file hash (MD5/SHA1/SHA256) against the Virushee multi-engine AV database. Returns detection ratio and per-engine results. No API key required.
- [watch_domain_ct](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Add a domain to Certificate Transparency monitoring. Alerts on new subdomains, suspicious patterns, wildcard certs, and more. Uses crt.sh for unlimited free CT log queries.
- [wayback_lookup](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:domain): Check the Wayback Machine (archive.org) for historical snapshots of a URL. Useful for tracking website changes or recovering deleted content.
- [wifi_investigation](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Investigate a wireless network by BSSID (MAC address) or SSID (network name). Returns OUI vendor lookup, MAC bit analysis (privacy/multicast), default SSID detection, WiGLE.net links, and security flags for rogue AP detection.
- [winreg_get_artifact](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Return the full body of a single Windows Registry forensic artifact by slug. Includes registry keys, description, forensic value, parsers, and MITRE mapping. Use winreg_list_artifacts first to discover slugs.
- [winreg_list_artifacts](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List Windows Registry forensic artifacts from the WinReg DFIR reference. Filter by category, hive, MITRE technique, or free-text keyword.
- [winreg_list_categories](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List the Windows Registry artifact categories in the WinReg DFIR reference. Returns category keys, names, descriptions, and artifact counts.
- [winreg_stats](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Return cache + manifest stats for the WinReg DFIR data: artifact counts, hive types, MITRE technique coverage, and LRU body-cache hit/miss ratios.
- [ws_add_connection](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Define a relationship between two subjects in a workspace.
- [ws_add_finding](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Log a finding with source, trust score, and confidence in a workspace.
- [ws_add_subject](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Register a subject (entity) in a workspace investigation.
- [ws_create](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Create a new investigation workspace for AEAD lifecycle tracking.
- [ws_export_stix](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Export workspace indicators as STIX 2.1 bundle or flat IOC list.
- [ws_exposure](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Calculate composite exposure score (0-100) for a target based on IOC reputation, breach exposure, infrastructure, attack surface, and threat intel.
- [ws_get](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get a workspace with all subjects, connections, findings, and timeline.
- [ws_list](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): List investigation workspaces. Each workspace is a full AEAD-lifecycle case with subjects, connections, findings, and timeline.
- [ws_render_graph](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Render an ASCII box-drawing relationship graph, timeline, or risk heatmap from workspace data.
- [ws_workflow_advance](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Advance a workspace to the next AEAD phase (Acquire→Enrich→Assess→Deliver→Complete).
- [ws_workflow_summary](https://pranithjain.qzz.io/mcp) (mcp:other): Get workspace summary: phase progress, findings breakdown, recommended commands.

## REST API (OpenAPI 3.1)

Spec: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/v1/openapi.json
Browser: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/docs (Scalar)
Health: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/v1/health
Feed status: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/api/v1/feed-status

## Open-source packages published from this codebase

- cti-text-extract - synchronous, dependency-free CTI entity extractor.
- stix21-builder - STIX 2.1 bundle builder with deterministic UUIDv5 IDs.
- cti-ioc-enrich - pluggable IOC enrichment framework.
- telegram-preview-parser - parse Telegram channel previews to structured JSON.
- deepdarkcti-parser - parse the fastfire/deepdarkCTI markdown index to typed JSON.
- cti-platform - the threat-intel platform as a standalone app.
- DFIR-PLATFORM - the DFIR toolkit as a standalone app.
- cti-stix-connector (Python) - ingests CSV/JSON/MyThreatIntel data into valid STIX 2.1.

## Live status

Per-feed freshness: https://pranithjain.qzz.io/status
Hourly cron-driven refresh; all caches have a 5-15 minute TTL.

---
Generated at build time. Pull /mcp-manifest.json for the authoritative tool list and 
/api/v1/openapi.json for the authoritative API spec.
